title: Chapter Eighty-One: Unification (Part Two)
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"Growing out your hair would be good."

Yeon-shin stared at the Desolate Sect Lord. She had made a jest. The absolute being who guarded the World Tree alone, whose image making jokes could hardly be imagined -- she had made a jest.

*No word from her should be taken lightly.*

He filed it. Grow the hair when becoming a master. He recalled crossing swords with local martial artists while searching for Hyeon Won-chang -- how he had learned hospitality by deliberately losing verbal fights, how the squad leaders called the black rank realm mastery. Three Flowers Gathered at Crown. Five Qi Return to Source. True qi responding to intent without delay.

By then no one would grab his hair in close combat.

A brief thought. When he met her green eyes again, the Sect Lord opened her mouth.

"Your expression is determined. Though it was idle talk."

A faint smile gathered at her lips. In the moonlight filtering through the great tree trunks, her beauty was not human. It was geological. Something laid down by centuries.

"Though martial artists under heaven make death their companion, Lightning Genius' intensity is not lacking. Not bad to see."

"I am honoured."

"Nothing to be grateful for. Nor sorry about." Her tone was gentle like a breeze. She continued. "The main fortress is not stingy with rewards. I called you to reward the merits achieved through your aspirations, so I shall bestow what you rightfully deserve."

*Finally.*

His heart moved. He tried to calm it and could not. Even a long exhale did nothing -- the flutter stayed. She would perceive everything, he knew. She always did. But she did not withdraw her smile.

"Your merits are very great. I shall speak of two things." She held up a slender hand, fingers spread. "The merit of finding the evil sect's headquarters that harmed civilians. The merit of greatly contributing to destroying the rebel group without sacrifice."

He said nothing. The former merit was just an incident that arose from the Seventh Apostle coveting his value. But the reward approaching this moment mattered more than pride.

"It can be said you have accumulated sufficient merit. Worthy of testing your qualification."

She extended her hand. Snow-white palm rising slowly. Then a very strange thing happened.

The moonlight behind the Sect Lord began being drawn to her hand. Not reflecting. *Bending.* Distorting like heat haze above summer stone, but cold, numinous, alive.

*This is not a human act.*

The great tree trunks surrounding the office released a gentle breeze. Leaves turned green in the full moon's dazzling breath. And there, above her palm -- a piece of light sprouted. Hazy. As if molded from moonlight itself.

"The World Tree's fruit," she said. "A small fragment."

It was like scooping up a handful of moon reflected in a lake.

Yeon-shin was silent. A stream of wind blowing in from the wall-less outside teased his neck as it passed. It was the most wondrous miracle of all he had seen in his life.

The vibration sound was mystical. He stared at the piece of light resting in the noble one's palm. Though it was his life's goal, he felt no surging energy. No internal force hammering at his meridians. Just a numinous feeling, vast and still, like standing at the edge of an ocean he could not see the far shore of.

"I know what is known to the world," she said. Her green eyes held his. Bottomless. Forest-deep. "All wrong. This is not a spirit medicine that bestows powerful strength."

"You spoke of qualification. Then what kind --"

"You must feel to know."

Her hand came slightly forward. The fragment closer now. The more he looked, the more it seemed not of this world.

"Try eating it."

He raised his hand reflexively. She slowly shook her head.

"It loses its power if separated from my body. You must bring your mouth."

*She speaks as if she herself is the World Tree.*

Countless questions bloomed. Was she connected by something spiritual? Was that what *guardian* truly meant? But he dared not speak them. He was in a position to receive whatever was bestowed.

*Senior Cheong Myeong said it. The information encountered changes as rank rises.*

He unhesitatingly lowered his head toward her hand. Slowly opened his mouth. The moment his lower lip, then the tip of his nose bridge, slightly touched her palm --

She withdrew it.

"The appearance is not good."

Calm. The fruit's fragment descended and gathered at her fingertips. Then she extended her hand again -- not toward his mouth. Toward his head. Straight to the upper dantian.

Her long finger touched Yeon-shin's brow.

Everything stopped.

His heart technique breathing cut off. His consciousness flew. A sensation like being buried in an abyss's swamp and endlessly elevating wrapped around all his intent and *soared.*

It was not a rise in power. It was experience. He felt the composition of air brushing his skin in detail, brought in nature's energy, became sensitive beyond imagination -- perception that felt like going mad and dying immediately.

Time slowed. He could see moonlight flowing.

In that moment, Jeong Yeon-shin became the Desolate Sect Lord.

He saw through her eyes. Gazed at himself -- a boy standing in a body made of pitch-black night, features mysterious, small. The world she perceived was infinite in its clarity. Every mote of dust a universe. Every breath an epoch.

*Would there be another miracle like this under heaven?*

That instant felt like countless eons. Her thin breath was clear, yet even one exhale had not finished. The wind brushing past his ears had not moved away. But the burn in his upper dantian was growing unbearable.

"Enough."

The word fell like a stone into water. Time regained its flow.

He staggered. A gentle hand gripped his shoulder.

"Not possible now," the Sect Lord said. "Too early."

He looked up. "What do you mean by too early?"

"Your realm is too shallow to accept the fruit. Devote yourself to training."

"Just now -- what exactly was that effect?"

"I tested if you could receive the World Tree's spirit nature. When your intent's vessel becomes firm, as long as the clan's tree does not wither, there will be no ascending to immortality through the Hundred Meetings point." She paused. "You would live eternally."

*Eternally.*

The word sat in the air between them. Spirit nature. Sharing the World Tree's divine qualities. Securing a lifeline beyond mortal reckoning.

He asked if his understanding was correct.

"Your interpretation is correct."

She slowly nodded. Rise to Divine Sword Group Leader's rank. Receive a complete fruit through worthy merits. Reach the realm of purple martial robes.

"It was inevitable," she said, "and."

Her touch gently pushed him back.

"A gift."

He stepped down from the tree trunk. All his senses had returned. But the peerless world he had experienced remained vivid -- carved into memory the way a blade carves stone.

So it was a test. And therefore a gift.

"Be victorious," she said. "Until you reach South Zhili."

She knew about the Namgung martial matches. Of course she did.

"May the Sect Lord also be at peace."

He paid his respects. The rustle of her clothes came clearly as she leaned back against the tree trunk, eyes slowly closing. He turned and walked away.

The stone doors closed behind him on their own.

Yeon-shin descended the stairs. Unlike going up, it felt very short. His feelings were strange. He had experienced something unimaginable, heard words of eternal life -- talk so far removed from his current self it might as well have been spoken in a dead language.

*Even if I receive the fruit.*

His realm was lacking. Mission experience creates advancement, builds merit. It meant returning to basics. Always basics.

The boy who came out of the Sect Lord's inner fortress moved his steps in silence. His shadow gradually lengthened in the moonlight.

Deep night was fading.

---
